title: Dense, Crowd-Pleasing Mexican Beef Carnitas
description: Tender, slow-cooked Mexican beef carnitas in a sweet and tangy glaze, perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe features a rich and flavorful sauce that complements the tender beef perfectly.

ingredients.name: 1 pound beef brisket, cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions: Heat the oil in a 5-quart Dutch oven or large clay cazuela over medium-high heat until it reaches 325°F.
Add the beef to the pot and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the diced onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the browned beef, orange juice, and any accumulated juices back to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven. Braise the beef for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and carefully skim the excess fat from the surface of the glaze.
Shred the beef with two forks and stir it back into the glaze.
Return the pot to the stovetop and simmer the glaze over medium heat until it has thickened and the beef is well coated.
Season the carnitas with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the carnitas hot, garnished with fresh cilantro and lime wedges if desired.
Simmer the glaze over low heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, to help it thicken and reduce.
Simmer the glaze over medium heat for 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, to help it thicken and reduce.
Simmer the glaze over medium heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, to help it thicken and reduce.
